task_completion.description: Verify that a channel named 'Lab_Report_Extractor' has been created in NextGen Connect.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Lab_Report_Extractor' in the Channels list or Dashboard of the NextGen Connect Administrator interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the channel source is configured to listen on TCP port 6661.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Source configuration screen showing 'TCP Listener' as the connector type and '6661' in the Port field.

task_completion.description: Verify the channel contains logic to extract the MRN from PID-3.1 and Base64 data from OBX-5.5, and decodes it.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Transformer, Filter, or Destination mapping script showing references to the PID segment (e.g., PID.3.1), OBX segment (e.g., OBX.5.5), and Base64 decoding functions (like FileUtil.decode or similar JavaScript/Java methods).

task_completion.description: Verify the destination is configured to write files to /home/ga/lab_reports/ with the correct naming convention.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Destination configuration screen showing 'File Writer' as the connector type, Directory set to '/home/ga/lab_reports', and File Name set to a pattern like '${MRN}_report.pdf' or similar variable substitution.

task_completion.description: Verify that the decoded PDF file was successfully generated in the target directory with the correct MRN.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal window or file explorer showing the file 'PAT78432_report.pdf' inside the '/home/ga/lab_reports/' directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with NextGen Connect (via GUI or REST API) and didn't just mock the configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of the actual NextGen Connect Administrator Java application or terminal windows executing curl/API scripts against localhost:8443.

integrity.description: Ensure the file was generated by the channel processing the message, not manually created.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the NextGen Connect Dashboard showing at least 1 'Received' and 'Sent' message for the 'Lab_Report_Extractor' channel. There should be no terminal commands manually touching or echoing into 'PAT78432_report.pdf'.

integrity.description: Ensure the output file is a genuine PDF decoded from the payload, not a dummy text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the contents of the generated file are displayed (e.g., via 'cat' or 'head' in the terminal), it must start with the PDF magic bytes '%PDF'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected channel name is 'Lab_Report_Extractor'. The source must listen on TCP port 6661. The output directory is '/home/ga/lab_reports'. The test HL7 message contains the MRN 'PAT78432' in PID-3.1, so the expected output filename is 'PAT78432_report.pdf'. The extracted file must be a valid PDF starting with '%PDF'.
